middleware_functions.name: protect
validation: ✅ Checks token present in Authorization header
✅ Checks user exists and is active
⚠️ Token error handling generic - doesn't distinguish between expired/invalid
middleware_functions.error_handling: ✅ 401 for missing/invalid token, 401 for inactive user
issues: No rate limiting on failed auth attempts
Removes password from user object but doesn't remove emailVerifyToken/resetPasswordToken consistently
Error message doesn't help debugging - 'Invalid or expired token' is same for both cases

middleware_functions.name: restrictTo
middleware_functions.validation: ✅ Checks user authenticated and has required role
middleware_functions.error_handling: ✅ 401 if not authenticated, 403 if role not permitted
issues: Returns detailed role info in error message (information disclosure risk)
No check for falsy roles
No audit logging of privilege denial

middleware/auth.js.summary: Basic but functional auth middleware. Main issues are lack of rate limiting and audit logging.

models/Attendance.js.summary: Basic structure works but has data type issues and missing indexes.

issues: ⚠️ Using Map for records/markedStudents (can have serialization issues with toObject)

models/ActiveSession.js.summary: Good structure but lacks TTL cleanup and has timezone/serialization concerns.

field_review.status: ⚠️ SHOULD BE Date type (currently string, no timezone)

issues: ❌ No validation: fromDate < toDate

models/LeaveApplication.js.summary: Missing crucial date validations. Dates should be Date type. Business logic mixed into model.

validation: ✅ Required fields: name, email, password, role
endpoints.error_handling: ✅ 400 for validation, 403 for role restriction, 500 for DB errors
issues: ⚠️ Email regex is too permissive (allows local-only emails)
⚠️ Password min length 6 is weak (should be 8+)
⚠️ No check for email verification token expiry
⚠️ Email send failure doesn't fail request (silent catch), user unaware

endpoints.error_handling: ✅ 401 for auth failures, special EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED status
issues: ⚠️ Error 'No account found with this email' allows user enumeration
⚠️ Generic 'Invalid email or password' better but would help here too
🔴 CRITICAL: No rate limiting on failed login attempts (brute force vulnerability)

endpoints.validation: ❌ No input validation on token format
issues: ⚠️ No token expiry check (any old token works forever)
⚠️ No rate limiting (could spam verify requests)
⚠️ No rate limiting (could spam verification emails)
⚠️ Email send failure swallowed silently
🔴 CRITICAL: No rate limiting on forgot-password (account takeover vector)
⚠️ Reset token expiry 3600000ms (1 hour) - could be longer for legitimate users
⚠️ Email send failure swallowed silently
⚠️ Weak password requirement (6 chars minimum)
⚠️ No rate limiting on reset attempts
⚠️ Token validity checked with $gt Date.now() but should also clear resetPasswordToken
✅ No issues identified
🔴🔴🔴 CRITICAL SECURITY VULNERABILITY 🔴🔴🔴
- Anyone can verify ANY email without authentication
- No rate limiting - attacker could verify bulk emails
- Returns success/failure revealing which emails exist (enumeration)
- Comment says 'Remove this route before college deployment!!' - MUST BE REMOVED
- This is a backdoor into the system
⚠️ Reveals admin existence (information disclosure)
✅ Useful for setup, probably okay to keep

critical_issues: 🔴 MANUAL VERIFY ENDPOINT IS CRITICAL SECURITY VULNERABILITY
🔴 NO RATE LIMITING on login/forgot-password/verify (brute force attacks)
⚠️ Email sent failure doesn't affect response (user doesn't know)
routes/auth.js.summary: Auth flow mostly solid but has critical vulnerabilities: manual verify endpoint, no rate limiting. Email verification logic is good but too permissive on failures.

issues: ⚠️ No pagination (could be slow with large user count)

validation: ✅ Prevents password update via this endpoint

issues: ⚠️ No validation that email.to is valid email format

⚠️ Failed sends counted but not retried

✅ Otherwise minimal and focused
routes/reports.js.summary: Simple implementation but lacks email validation. Only used for notifications, low risk.

critical_issues.description: Anyone can verify any email without authentication. No rate limiting. Allows unauthorized access to system. Comment says 'Remove before college deployment!!'
critical_issues.fix: DELETE THIS ENDPOINT IMMEDIATELY. It's a critical security vulnerability.

critical_issues.description: GET /api/users/student/:usn is defined twice, causing undefined behavior and routing conflicts
critical_issues.fix: Remove duplicate definition. Keep one, verify route order is correct.

critical_issues.description: end-session route uses 'updatedSession' which is never defined. Should be 'session'. Will crash when save=true.
critical_issues.fix: Change 'updatedSession' to 'session' on lines where it's used in end-session endpoint.

critical_issues.issue: AUTHORIZATION BYPASS: Manager can modify any teacher's attendance
critical_issues.description: Manager can end and save ANY teacher's session, creating attendance records. Should only affect own sessions.
critical_issues.fix: Add check to prevent manager from creating attendance from teacher sessions, or limit manager access.

critical_issues.description: DELETE /api/leaves/:id allows ANY authenticated user to delete ANY leave application
critical_issues.fix: Add authorization check: only student who created it or manager can delete

critical_issues.description: POST /api/leaves accepts future dates, past dates, and fromDate > toDate without validation
critical_issues.fix: Validate: fromDate < toDate, both dates in future, proper date format

critical_issues.description: No protection against brute force attacks on login, forgot-password, register endpoints. Vulnerable to credential stuffing and password guessing.
critical_issues.fix: Implement rate limiting middleware (e.g., express-rate-limit): 5 attempts per 15 minutes per IP

critical_issues.issue: AUTHORIZATION: Teacher can edit any attendance record
critical_issues.description: PUT /api/attendance/:id allows teacher to edit any attendance, not just their own
critical_issues.fix: Add check: teacher can only edit attendance for their own classes

warnings.description: Creating class has no validation. Required fields like name, subject, branch can be missing or empty
warnings.fix: Validate all required fields before creating class

warnings.description: Any authenticated user can update any other user's profile
warnings.fix: Add check: user can only update own profile, or admin/manager can update others

warnings.issue: Dates stored as strings instead of Date type
warnings.description: fromDate and toDate are strings, not Date objects. No timezone handling, no date comparisons possible
warnings.fix: Change to: fromDate: { type: Date, required: true }, toDate: { type: Date, required: true }

warnings.description: date field is YYYY-MM-DD string, should be Date for proper timezone handling and queries

warnings.issue: No authorization on GET /student/:usn and GET /teacher/:teacherId
warnings.description: Any user can query any student's or teacher's attendance without permission
warnings.fix: Add role checks: student can only see own, teacher can only see own classes, admin/manager all

warnings.description: GET /verify-email/:token works forever. No expiry check on the token
warnings.fix: Add timestamp when token created, reject if > 24 hours old

warnings.issue: No TTL cleanup for old sessions
warnings.description: Old completed sessions accumulate in database forever. Should auto-delete after retention period
warnings.fix: Add TTL index: activeSessionSchema.index({ createdAt: 1 }, { expireAfterSeconds: 604800 })

warnings.issue: Start session deletes previous session silently
warnings.description: If teacher starts new session without ending previous, old session is deleted without confirmation
warnings.fix: Either warn teacher or prevent starting new session while one is active

warnings.description: If email fails to send, user is told success but never receives verification link
warnings.fix: Either retry email or return error so user knows there's a problem

warnings.description: Batch delete doesn't delete parent accounts properly or leave applications
warnings.fix: Delete all leave applications for students in batch before deleting students

warnings.description: In POST /mark, lat/lng received from client without validating they're in valid ranges (-90~90, -180~180)

warnings.issue: fileData stored as base64 string in database
warnings.description: Could be very large, slowing down database. Should use ObjectId reference to file storage
warnings.fix: Consider using cloud storage (S3, GCS) or separate file collection

recommendations.recommendation: Delete manual-verify endpoint and manual testing routes before production deployment
Fix undefined 'updatedSession' variable in end-session endpoint (line 251)
Add comprehensive authorization checks to all endpoints. Document who can access what.
Implement rate limiting on: login (5/15min), forgot-password (3/hour), register (10/hour), verify-email (5/hour)
recommendations.impact: Prevents brute force and spam attacks

recommendations.recommendation: Add comprehensive input validation to all POST/PUT endpoints. Validate: required fields, data types, ranges, format.
recommendations.impact: Prevents invalid data and some injection attacks

recommendations.recommendation: Convert all date strings to Date type in models (Attendance.date, LeaveApplication.fromDate/toDate)
recommendations.impact: Enables proper date queries and timezone handling

recommendations.recommendation: Add try-catch for all socket.io operations. Log failures. Don't silently continue on errors.
Add TTL index to ActiveSession for auto-cleanup: db.activesessions.createIndex({ createdAt: 1 }, { expireAfterSeconds: 86400 })
Fix route ordering in users.js: specific routes before generic routes. Remove duplicates.
Add audit logging for: user creation/deletion, attendance modifications, leave approvals, authorization failures
recommendations.impact: Better security audit trail and debugging

recommendations.recommendation: Add email verification on schema level using mongoose-validator or custom validation
Standardize date/time handling: use UTC Date objects throughout, not locale strings or date strings

recommendations.impact: Prevents slow queries on large datasets

recommendations.recommendation: Use @joi or zod schemas for comprehensive input validation instead of inline checks
Add CORS whitelist instead of '*', implement CSRF protection, add security headers

summary.overallRisk: 🔴 HIGH - Critical vulnerabilities found. Should not deploy without fixes.
keyFindings: 1. Manual verify endpoint is a security backdoor - DELETE IMMEDIATELY
2. Undefined variable bug in attendance end-session will crash
3. Multiple authorization bypass vulnerabilities (delete leaves, edit any attendance)
4. No rate limiting enables brute force attacks
5. Date validation missing on leave applications
6. Duplicate route definition causes routing issues
7. Missing input validation on multiple endpoints
summary.deploymentReadiness: ❌ NOT READY FOR PRODUCTION - Fix critical issues first
summary.estimatedFixTime: 2-3 hours for critical issues, 6-8 hours for all warnings
